017package org.apache.juneau.commons.settings;
018
019import java.util.*;
020
021/**
022 * A {@link PropertySource} decorator that adds <b>relaxed binding</b> over a delegate source.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * When a property lookup misses against the delegate, this source retries with a small set of
026 * <i>canonical name variants</i> derived from the requested name, so a single logical key can be supplied in any of
027 * the common environment conventions and still resolve.  This is the mechanism that lets a Kubernetes-style
028 * environment variable such as {@code MY_SECTION_MY_KEY} satisfy a lookup for {@code MySection/myKey}, or
029 * {@code MY_PROP} satisfy {@code my.prop} / {@code myProp}.
030 *
031 * <h5 class='section'>Candidate order</h5>
032 * <p>
033 * The requested name is always tried <b>verbatim first</b>, so exact-match behavior is preserved unchanged (an
034 * already-canonical key resolves with no transformation, and a source that defines the literal name wins).  Only on a
035 * miss are the relaxed variants tried, in this order:
036 * <ol>
037 *    <li>verbatim (the requested name, unchanged);
038 *    <li><b>upper underscore</b> &mdash; every run of non-alphanumeric characters (and every lower&rarr;upper camel-case
039 *       boundary) becomes a single {@code _}, then the whole thing is upper-cased.  This is the dominant env-var form:
040 *       {@code my.prop}/{@code my-prop}/{@code myProp}/{@code MySection/myKey} &rarr; {@code MY_PROP} /
041 *       {@code MY_SECTION_MY_KEY};
042 *    <li><b>lower dotted</b> &mdash; the same separator collapse but joined with {@code .} and lower-cased
043 *       ({@code MY_PROP} &rarr; {@code my.prop}), so a dotted-property lookup can be satisfied by an env-style key and
044 *       vice-versa.
045 * </ol>
046 *
047 * <p>
048 * Because the boundary between "section" and "key" in a flat relaxed name (e.g. the underscores in
049 * {@code MY_SECTION_MY_KEY}) is inherently ambiguous, this decorator deliberately works in the
050 * <b>lookup-key &rarr; candidate-name</b> direction only (the well-defined direction): the caller asks for a known
051 * logical key and the decorator generates the environment-convention spellings to probe.  It never tries to reverse a
052 * flat env name back into a {@code section/key} pair.
053 *
054 * <h5 class='section'>Usage</h5>
055 * <p class='bjava'>
056 *    <jc>// Wrap the system-env source so env vars bind relaxedly.</jc>
057 *    <jv>settings</jv> = Settings.<jsm>create</jsm>().addSource(<jk>new</jk> RelaxedPropertySource(Settings.<jsf>SYSTEM_ENV_SOURCE</jsf>)).build();
058 * </p>
059 *
060 * @since 10.0.0
061 */
062public class RelaxedPropertySource implements PropertySource {
063
064   private final PropertySource delegate;
065
066   /**
067    * Constructor.
068    *
069    * @param delegate The wrapped source that the generated candidate names are probed against.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
070    */
071   public RelaxedPropertySource(PropertySource delegate) {
072      this.delegate = Objects.requireNonNull(delegate, "delegate");
073   }
074
075   @Override
076   public PropertyLookupResult get(String name) {
077      if (name == null)
078         return PropertyLookupResult.missing();
079      for (var candidate : candidates(name)) {
080         var r = delegate.get(candidate);
081         if (r.isPresent())
082            return r;
083      }
084      return PropertyLookupResult.missing();
085   }
086
087   /**
088    * Generates the ordered, de-duplicated list of candidate names for a requested name (verbatim first).
089    *
090    * @param name The requested property name.
091    * @return The candidate names to probe, in priority order.
092    */
093   static List<String> candidates(String name) {
094      var out = new ArrayList<String>(3);
095      out.add(name);
096      var tokens = tokenize(name);
097      if (! tokens.isEmpty()) {
098         addIfNew(out, String.join("_", tokens).toUpperCase(Locale.ROOT));
099         addIfNew(out, String.join(".", tokens).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT));
100      }
101      return out;
102   }
103
104   /**
105    * Splits a name into lowercase-normalized word tokens, breaking on any non-alphanumeric run and on each
106    * lower&rarr;upper camel-case boundary.  E.g. {@code "MySection/myKey"} &rarr; {@code [my, section, my, key]}.
107    */
108   private static List<String> tokenize(String name) {
109      var tokens = new ArrayList<String>();
110      var sb = new StringBuilder();
111      char prev = 0;
112      for (var i = 0; i < name.length(); i++) {
113         var c = name.charAt(i);
114         if (! Character.isLetterOrDigit(c)) {
115            // Separator (., -, /, _, space, …): flush the current token.
116            if (! sb.isEmpty()) {
117               tokens.add(sb.toString());
118               sb.setLength(0);
119            }
120         } else {
121            // camelCase boundary: lower/digit followed by upper starts a new token.
122            if (! sb.isEmpty() && Character.isUpperCase(c) && (Character.isLowerCase(prev) || Character.isDigit(prev))) {
123               tokens.add(sb.toString());
124               sb.setLength(0);
125            }
126            sb.append(Character.toLowerCase(c));
127         }
128         prev = c;
129      }
130      if (! sb.isEmpty())
131         tokens.add(sb.toString());
132      return tokens;
133   }
134
135   private static void addIfNew(List<String> out, String candidate) {
136      if (! out.contains(candidate))
137         out.add(candidate);
138   }
139}
